data.title: Bridgewater China backs Chinese equities, gold after March fund drawdown
data.description:
By Summer Zhen

Bridgewater China sees resilience in Chinese equities continuing and is tilted bullish on gold, even after its onshore funds suffered a record drawdown in March, according to a letter to investors reviewed by Reuters.

The market view and outlook from the Chinese unit of the world's largest hedge fund are closely watched and provide global investors with signals on macro positioning and asset allocation amid disruptions brought on by the Iran war.

"Chinese assets held up better than most global counterparts" amid a market rout triggered by the conflict, thanks to its continued energy reserve investment, Bridgewater (China) Investment Management said in the letter sent in April.

China's energy reserves and low inflation stand it in good stead to resist further shocks, and Beijing retains "ample policy room, tools and capability" to respond if things get worse, the letter said.

The relative outperformance of Chinese assets over the past few months is drawing fund managers' attention.

The fund did, however, shift to a more neutral stance on Chinese bonds due to imported inflation risks.

It is slightly overweight on gold, arguing short-term factors have been dragging on the price recently and that long-term concerns over the credibility of fiat currencies and sovereign debt should be supportive.

The fund's flagship yuan-denominated All Weather Plus No.3 fund, designed to withstand volatility, lost 6.4% in March before fees, the biggest monthly drop since its launch in 2021, according to the letter.

That trimmed the fund's first-quarter gains to 4.2%, the letter showed. During the period, the Shanghai Composite lost 1.9% and global stocks EURONEXT:IACWI fell 3.5% as soaring energy prices hit nearly every asset class.

Bridgewater China declined to comment to a Reuters email on the fund's performance and market view.

The fund invests in Chinese stocks, bonds and commodities. Other tranches of All Weather Plus funds posted similar results, according to investors.

The All Weather Plus funds have rebounded strongly so far in April, per investors familiar with the performance.

data.title: Netherlands' Cabka Q1 sales rise 7%, profitability improves
data.description:
Overview

  • Netherlands plastic recycler's Q1 2026 sales rose 7% yr/yr to €47.3 mln

  • Profitability improved on disciplined cost management and strategy execution

  • Company reiterates 2026 guidance for revenue and EBITDA margin improvement

Outlook

  • Cabka reiterates 2026 guidance, expecting improved revenues and higher EBITDA margin versus 2025

  • Company says order intake across segments developed positively, providing good visibility for Q2

  • Cabka remains focused on disciplined execution and cash generation amid volatile geopolitical environment

Result Drivers

  • EUROPEAN GROWTH - Portfolio segment revenues in Europe rose 10% yr/yr, while Contract Manufacturing in Europe grew 32% yr/yr

  • US PORTFOLIO DECLINE - Portfolio revenues in the US fell 18% yr/yr, in line with expectations, as US operations shift focus toward Contract Manufacturing

  • DISCIPLINED COST MANAGEMENT - Improved profitability attributed to ongoing cost discipline and strategy execution

Company press release:

Key Details

Metric

Q1 Revenue

Beat/Miss

Actual

EUR 47.30 mln

Consensus Estimate

Analyst Coverage

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 3 "strong buy" or "buy", no "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell"

  • The average consensus recommendation for the non-paper containers & packaging peer group is "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Cabka NV is €2.70, about 36.4% above its April 20 closing price of €1.98

data.title: Viking Line Provides No Forecast For 2026
data.description:
Viking Line Abp :

  • VIKING LINE Q1 EBIT EUR -18.8 MILLION

  • VIKING LINE Q1 PRETAX PROFIT EUR -19.5 MILLION

  • VIKING LINE Q1 NET INCOME EUR -19.5 MILLION

  • IS CURRENTLY NOT PROVIDING ANY FORECAST FOR FULL-YEAR RESULT FOR 2026

  • ASSESS THAT MARKET CONDITIONS WILL CONTINUE TO BE CHARACTERISED BY UNCERTAINTY AND SUBDUED DEMAND, ESPECIALLY REGARDING DEVELOPMENT OF FUEL PRICES

  • BOD PREVIOUSLY ASSESSED THAT 2026 PROFIT BEFORE TAX WOULD BE IN LINE WITH OR SLIGHTLY BETTER THAN IN 2025 BUT IN LIGHT OF THE INCREASED VOLATILITY IN ENERGY PRICES AND RELATED COSTS, BOD CONSIDERS THAT THESE OUTLOOKS ARE NO LONGER APPLICABLE

data.title: UK wage growth cools by less than expected in 3 months to February
data.description:
British wage growth, excluding bonuses, slowed by slightly less than expected to 3.6% in annual terms in the three months to February, the Office for National Statistics said on Tuesday.

Economists polled by Reuters had mostly expected regular average weekly earnings growth of 3.5%, down from 3.8% in the previous month.

Until the outbreak of the Iran war at the end of February, the Bank of England had been showing increasing concern about weaker hiring.

But new inflation pressures have emerged, caused by the jump in energy prices due to the war in the Middle East, and economists have pushed back their forecasts for when the BoE is likely resume cutting borrowing costs.

Officials at the central bank say the weak economic backdrop is likely to make it harder for workers to demand bigger pay increases in response to the expected rise in inflation.

data.title: Spanish stocks - Factors to watch on April 21
data.description:
The following Spanish stocks may be affected by newspaper reports and other factors on Tuesday. Reuters has not verified the newspaper reports, and cannot vouch for their accuracy:

ENAGAS

Spanish gas grid operator Enagas said on Tuesday it has reached an agreement with Singapore sovereign wealth fund GIC to buy a 31.5% stake in French gas grid operator Terega.

Separately, the company said it completed the sale of a 40% stake in its renewables unit Enagas Renovable to Hy24 for 48 million euros.

Enagas also reported Q1 net profit of 56.9 million euros.

IBERDROLA , ENDESA

The CEOs of utilities Iberdrola and Endesa on Monday defended the operation of their companies' power plants during the unprecedented blackout that plunged the Iberian Peninsula into the dark last year.

NEINOR HOMES

Spanish homebuilder Neinor Homes said on Monday that CEO Borja García-Egotxeaga would step down at the end of 2026, to be succeeded by the company's current Chief Financial Officer Jordi Argemi.

MERLIN PROPERTIES

The company said on Monday that the senior executive and executive director Miguel Ollero passed away. Ollero acted as chief operating and chief financial officer of the company.

PUIG , ESTEE LAUDER

Estee Lauder has asked J.P. Morgan to structure about 5 billion euros of financing for a potential takeover bid for Spain's Puig, Spanish newspaper Expansion reported on Tuesday, citing sources.

SQUIRREL MEDIA

Squirrel Media said on Monday it has completed the acquisition of Grupo Green Shark and that the integration will be rolled out progressively over the coming months.

CELLNEX

Berenberg cut its target price on Cellnex to 38 euros from 46.
